question: Verify the abbreviations related to the NRC and explain NuScale's NRC licensing approach through the text.

nuscale_ch01_0859.title: SRP 19.3, Rev 0: Regulatory Treatment of Non-Safety Systems for Passive Advanced Light Water ReactorsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0859.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various sections of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) related to risk and safety for passive advanced light water reactors. It notes that NuScale fully conforms to SRP 19.3 regarding the regulatory treatment of non-safety systems, while partial or full conformance is indicated for other SRP sections, with specific exceptions and justifications provided where requirements do not apply to the NuScale design. The table documents how NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report aligns with regulatory criteria for licensing and safety.

nuscale_ch01_0129.title: 1.9.4 Operational Experience (Generic Communications)
nuscale_ch01_0129.summary: NuScale reviewed and assessed conformance with the Standard Review Plan (SRP), associated guidance, and NRC-developed Design-Specific Review Standards (DSRS) for its small modular reactor design, documenting findings in referenced tables and reports. The company also evaluated compliance with Three Mile Island (TMI) requirements and addressed unresolved and generic safety issues per regulatory requirements, referencing the NRC Generic Issues Program and relevant NUREG documents.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis report diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and operations, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for flow, pressure, temperature, and level measurement devices, as well as sample probes, analysis accessories, and burner monitoring equipment, ensuring consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0858.title: SRP 19.2, Initial Issuance: Review of Risk Information Used to Support Permanent PlantSpecific Changes to the Licensing Basis: General GuidanceAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0858.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various sections of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) related to risk information and safety criteria for licensing basis changes. It notes that NuScale’s design generally conforms to SRP requirements, with some exceptions for criteria specific to other reactor types (such as BWR containment venting and spent fuel pool mitigation, which are not applicable due to NuScale’s below-grade SFP design). The table documents the conformance status and any relevant comments for each SRP section reviewed.

nuscale_ch01_0076.title: Conformance with Standard Review Plan
nuscale_ch01_0076.summary: NuScale reviewed the Standard Review Plan (SRP), including relevant guidance and acceptance criteria, and documented any gaps in a summary report submitted to the NRC. The company also assessed conformance with NRC-developed guidance specific to small modular reactors and addressed applicable Three Mile Island (TMI) and generic safety issues as required by regulations, with resolutions managed through the NRC Generic Issues Program. Relevant information and compliance details are incorporated into referenced tables and FSAR sections.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0863.title: Table 1.9-8: Conformance with SECY-93-087, "Policy, Technical, and Licensing Issues Pertaining to Evolutionary and Advanced Light-Water Reactor Designs"
nuscale_ch01_0863.summary: Table 1.9-8 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SECY-93-087 policy and technical issues for advanced light-water reactor designs. For each issue, the table indicates whether the NuScale design conforms, partially conforms, or is not applicable, with brief comments and references to relevant FSAR sections. Most criteria are met or not applicable, with partial conformance noted for ATWS and hydrogen control due to specific design approaches.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used for identifying and numbering tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that sensitive material is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements, while all such content is clearly marked in the non-public version.

nuscale_ch01_0130.title: 1.9.5 Advanced and Evolutionary Light-Water Reactor Design Issues
nuscale_ch01_0130.summary: This section explains that the NuScale design incorporates relevant operational experience and complies with regulatory criteria, including NRC Bulletins, Generic Letters, and 10 CFR 21 notifications, as part of its safety and supplier evaluation processes. It also addresses advanced and evolutionary light-water reactor design issues, following NRC guidance and policy documents such as SECY-93-087 and the Standard Review Plan (SRP).

nuscale_ch01_0012.title: Page Numbering
nuscale_ch01_0012.summary: This section explains the conventions used for numbering pages, tables, and figures in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. Pages are numbered sequentially within each section, with appendices using a chapter and letter format (e.g., 3A-1), while tables and figures are numbered according to their section and placed at the end of each section. The section also notes that sensitive information is withheld from the public version of the report, in accordance with regulatory requirements.
